title: myriophyllum pedunculatum subsp. pedunculatum
intro: Myriophyllum pedunculatum subsp. pedunculatum, also known as the common water-milfoil, is a submerged aquatic plant species that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its ability to reduce inflammation and exhibit antioxidant activities. The health benefits of M. pedunculatum subsp. pedunculatum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its possible anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of this plant may include infusions, teas, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
